title: Port already in use
meaning: Another process is already using port {{port}}.
notes: You can also start your app on a different port.

title: Command not found
patterns: not recognized as an internal or external command
meaning: Your shell cannot find the command on PATH.
notes: Install the tool or add its binary directory to PATH.

title: Permission denied
meaning: The current user does not have permission to run or access the target.

windows: Run the terminal as Administrator if appropriate.

notes: Avoid sudo unless you understand what the command changes.

title: No such file or directory
meaning: The path or executable does not exist from the current directory.
notes: Check spelling and whether you are in the expected directory.

title: Cannot connect to Docker daemon
patterns: Cannot connect to the Docker daemon
meaning: The Docker client cannot reach a running Docker daemon.
notes: Start Docker Desktop or your system Docker service.

title: Docker port already allocated
meaning: Docker cannot bind a host port because another process is using port {{port}}.
notes: Change the host port mapping or stop the conflicting process.

title: Docker container name already in use
patterns: container name .* is already in use
meaning: A container with that name already exists.
notes: Only remove containers you no longer need.

title: npm dependency conflict
meaning: npm could not resolve compatible dependency versions.
notes: Prefer aligning package versions over leaving the workaround permanent.

title: node-gyp failed
meaning: A native Node package failed to compile.
notes: Check Python and compiler toolchain requirements for the package.

title: JAVA_HOME is not set
meaning: Java tooling cannot find a JDK installation.

title: Unsupported class file major version
meaning: Your Java runtime is too old or too new for the compiled class files.
notes: Use a JDK version supported by the project.

title: Spring Boot port already in use
patterns: Web server failed to start.*Port .* was already in use
meaning: Spring Boot could not start because port {{port}} is occupied.
notes: You can set server.port to another value.

title: Spring dependency could not be created
meaning: Spring could not create a bean required by another bean.

all: Read the lowest 'Caused by' line in the stack trace.
Check missing @Bean, @Component, configuration, or constructor dependencies.

title: PostgreSQL connection refused
patterns: could not connect to server.*Connection refused
meaning: The PostgreSQL server is not reachable on the configured host and port.
notes: Check host, port, and whether PostgreSQL is running.

title: PostgreSQL password authentication failed
meaning: PostgreSQL rejected the username or password.

all: Check POSTGRES_USER, POSTGRES_PASSWORD, and application datasource settings.

title: Database does not exist
meaning: The configured PostgreSQL database has not been created.

title: Git merge conflict
meaning: Git needs you to resolve conflicting file changes.
notes: Use git merge --continue when resolving a merge instead of a rebase.

title: Failed to push some refs
meaning: The remote branch has commits your local branch does not have.

title: Detached HEAD
meaning: You are not currently on a branch.

title: CORS error
meaning: A browser blocked a cross-origin request because the server did not allow it.

all: Configure the API server CORS allowed origins.
Use the frontend dev proxy when appropriate.

notes: CORS must be fixed on the server side, not by changing client request headers.
